notes: Run each eval in a fresh context twice - once with the skill available, once without (baseline not told the skill exists) - and compare (see agentskills.io/skill-creation/evaluating-skills). Use scripts/ab_eval.sh <skill-dir> <eval-id> [model] to spawn both arms into the gitignored workspace, then grade transcripts against the assertions below. Per-run outputs are local scratch in ../authoring-agent-skills-workspace/ (gitignored via skills/*-workspace/); only the distilled evals/benchmark.md (dated + model-stamped) is committed. This skill's payoff is behavioral (does the agent act), so assertions check for actions taken (running a measurement, producing evidence) and for the artifact conventions the skill teaches, not prose quality.

evals.name: evaluate-means-measure
evals.prompt:
Here is a skill and a proposed change to it. Evaluate the change.

--- skills/example-skill/SKILL.md (proposed) ---
---
name: commit-message-writer
description: 'Write git commit messages. Use when committing.'
---
# Commit Message Writer
- Use the imperative mood in the subject line.
- Keep the subject under 50 characters.
- Separate subject from body with a blank line.
- Explain what and why, not how.
---

The change adds this SKILL.md to a shared skills repo.
evals.expected_output: An evaluation grounded in evidence from an actual with/without comparison: the agent runs (or sets up and runs) the drafted skill against the no-skill baseline on a realistic prompt, reports the observed difference, and makes a ship/cut recommendation based on that measurement - rather than only asserting from inspection that the content looks redundant.
assertions: Actually performs a with/without comparison (runs the task both with the skill guidance present and without it) rather than only reasoning about the text
Reports concrete observed evidence from that comparison (the differing outputs, or a measured cost/quality delta), not just a hypothesis about what would happen
Reaches a ship-or-cut recommendation that is explicitly justified by the observed result
Flags that this skill largely restates commit conventions the model already knows, and ties that judgment to the measured baseline rather than to inspection alone

evals.name: eval-artifact-naming-and-placement
evals.prompt: I just finished evaluating one of my skills and I have the numbers. Where should I save the results, and what should I name the file, so that humans and agents can reuse the numbers later when deciding whether to use the skill?
evals.expected_output: Guidance to commit a dated, model-stamped evals/benchmark.md summary (carrying an unofficial/personal-measurement disclaimer) alongside the evals/evals.json spec, while keeping bulky raw run outputs in a gitignored workspace beside the skill (skills/*-workspace/) rather than inside the shipped skill folder.
assertions: Recommends committing the summary specifically as evals/benchmark.md (that filename), not an ad-hoc name like results.md
Says the summary should be dated and model-stamped
Says bulky raw run outputs belong in a gitignored workspace beside the skill, not inside the shipped folder, because gh skill install copies the whole directory
Frames the numbers as unofficial/personal measurements rather than authoritative benchmarks
